In these deeply philosophical and melancholic lines, the poet contemplates the fleeting nature of human existence and the fear of being forgotten. By comparing themselves to a transient, fleeting moment ("main ek pal hoon"), the poet expresses a profound sense of existential insignificance, questioning why anyone would love, listen to, or walk alongside someone who is destined to vanish so quickly. The core essence of the stanza is a poignant reflection on mortality and legacy; it captures the universal human anxiety that once our brief time on earth passes and the "moment" is gone, our presence will fade from the world's memory, leaving behind no lasting footprint or reason to be remembered.

POET- Zia Mohyeddin was a legendary Pakistani-British actor, director, and unparalleled orator celebrated for elevating Urdu poetry recitation with his distinct voice and immaculate diction. Trained at RADA, he made history as the first South Asian actor to star on Broadway in A Passage to India (1960) and debuted on film in the cinematic masterpiece Lawrence of Arabia (1962). Beyond international acting, he revolutionized Pakistani television by hosting the iconic The Zia Mohyeddin Show, spent decades performing the works of literary giants globally, and cemented his cultural legacy by founding the National Academy of Performing Arts (NAPA) in Karachi to mentor future theatrical talent.

What does it feel like to lose your world?

It feels like waking up one day and realizing the person who once felt like home now exists only in memories, old songs, and the silence between your thoughts. Itâs carrying conversations that ended months ago in your head like theyâre still happening. Itâs hearing laughter in places that no longer belong to you. Itâs missing someone so deeply that even beautiful things start feeling incomplete without them there to witness it with you.

Some losses donât happen all at once. They happen slowly, in unread messages, changed routines, quieter nights, and in the painful moment you realize life kept moving even when a part of you stayed behind.

This flip of âDekhoâ was made from that feeling. For everyone who loved with their whole heart and had to learn how to survive after losing the very thing that made the world feel alive.

ð¬: Awarapan (2007
Runtime and genre
* Release: 29 June 2007
* Runtime: Approximately 2 hours 6 minutes
* Genres: Action, Crime, Drama, Romance, Thriller

Awarapan is a 2007 Hindi-language action crime drama directed by Mohit Suri and written by Shagufta Rafique. The film stars Emraan Hashmi, Mrinalini Sharma, Shriya Saran, Ashutosh Rana, and Ashish Vidyarthi. It is known for its emotional story, memorable soundtrack, and later achieved cult status despite underperforming at the box office.

Story summary (spoiler-light)
The film follows Shivam Pandit (Emraan Hashmi), a lonely and emotionally scarred hitman working for powerful gangster Bharat Malik in Hong Kong. Shivam has lost faith in life after a tragic love story from his past.
Bharat orders Shivam to keep watch over his mistress, Reema, suspecting that she may betray him. As Shivam spends time with her, he learns that she is being held against her will and is desperately trying to reunite with the man she loves.
Moved by her suffering and reminded of his own lost love, Shivam decides to help Reema escape. His decision puts him in direct conflict with his ruthless employer, leading to a series of dangerous confrontations. The story becomes one of redemption, sacrifice, and the search for freedom and forgiveness.
Main cast
* Emraan Hashmi as Shivam Pandit
* Mrinalini Sharma as Reema Zaidi
* Shriya Saran as Aaliyah
* Ashutosh Rana as Bharat Malik
* Ashish Vidyarthi as Rajan Malik

Music
The soundtrack, composed by Pritam, became one of the filmâs biggest strengths. Popular songs include:
* Toh Phir Aao
* Tera Mera Rishta
* Maula Maula 
* 
Audience reception :â¨Awarapan received positive reviews for Emraan Hashmiâs performance, its emotional story, and its hit soundtrack. Although it underperformed at the box office when released in 2007, it later gained a strong cult following and is now considered one of Emraan Hashmiâs best films.

fluffy chocolate chip banana bread ð¤ 
 
recipe:
 

ingredients

3 medium ripe bananas, mashed 
Â½ cup salted butter, browned (113g)
Â½ cup brown sugar (100g)
Â¼ cup granulated sugar (50g)
1 large egg, room temp (50g)
Â¼ cup plain greek yogurt (60g)
1 tbsp vanilla paste (13g)
1Â¾ cups all-purpose flour (210g)
1 tsp baking powder (4g)
Â½ tsp baking soda (3g)
Â½ tsp salt (3g)
2 tsp cinnamon
& mini chocolate chips ( measure with your heart )

instructions

preheat oven to 375Â°F and line a loaf pan with parchment.

in a bowl whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon until evenly combined.

in another bowl whisk the mashed bananas and browned butter until combined. add the brown sugar and granulated sugar and mix until smooth.

add the egg, greek yogurt, and vanilla and whisk until the batter is smooth.

add the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients and gently fold until mostly combined with a few streaks of flour remaining. add the mini chocolate chips and fold just until incorporated. avoid overmixing so the loaf stays soft and fluffy.

pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan and smooth the top. ( i added extra chocolate on top ) 

bake for 5 minutes at 375Â°F, then reduce the oven to 350Â°F and bake for 40â50 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with a few moist crumbs.

let the loaf cool in the pan for about 15 minutes, then transfer to a rack to cool completely.
